usage_notes: Used in scientific, academic, and everyday contexts to describe a structured test
common_collocations: ein Experiment durchführen, ein Experiment wiederholen, das Experiment scheitert
synonyms: der Versuch, die Untersuchung, der Test
image_search_query: scientist laboratory experiment test tubes research
image_context: A scientist in a lab coat conducting an experiment with glass equipment
example_1: Das Experiment lieferte überraschende Ergebnisse.
example_1_translation: O experimento produziu resultados surpreendentes.
example_2: Sie führten das Experiment unter kontrollierten Bedingungen durch.
example_2_translation: Eles realizaram o experimento em condições controladas.
example_3: Das Experiment bestätigte die ursprüngliche Hypothese.
example_3_translation: O experimento confirmou a hipótese original.
pronunciation_tips: Latin origin; stress on third syllable: ex-pe-ri-MENT; final 't' is pronounced
memory_hook: EXPERIment = your EXPERIENCE of testing an idea
related_words: experimentieren, der Experimentator, das Versuchslabor, die Kontrolle

usage_notes: Used in scientific and academic contexts; implies an unverified proposition subject to testing
common_collocations: eine Hypothese aufstellen, eine Hypothese testen, die Hypothese bestätigen
synonyms: die Annahme, die These, die Theorie
image_search_query: scientist laboratory hypothesis testing whiteboard research
image_context: A scientist writing a hypothesis on a whiteboard in a laboratory
example_1: Der Forscher stellt eine neue Hypothese auf.
example_1_translation: O pesquisador apresenta uma nova hipótese.
example_2: Die Hypothese wurde durch Experimente bestätigt.
example_2_translation: A hipótese foi confirmada por experimentos.
example_3: Ohne Daten bleibt es nur eine Hypothese.
example_3_translation: Sem dados, continua sendo apenas uma hipótese.
pronunciation_tips: Greek origin; stress on third syllable: hy-po-THE-se; 'y' = /y/ (like French 'u')
memory_hook: HYPOthesis = below the level of proof; still needs testing
related_words: die Theorie, das Experiment, beweisen, die Forschung

primary_meaning: molecule, smallest unit of a chemical compound
usage_notes: Technical term in chemistry and biology; refers to the smallest particle retaining chemical properties
common_collocations: ein Molekül besteht aus, das Molekül verbindet sich, komplexe Moleküle
image_search_query: molecule chemical structure 3D model atoms bonds
image_context: A 3D model of a molecule showing atoms connected by chemical bonds
example_1: Ein Wassermolekül besteht aus zwei Wasserstoffatomen und einem Sauerstoffatom.
example_1_translation: Uma molécula de água é composta de dois átomos de hidrogênio e um átomo de oxigênio.
example_2: Die Forscher analysierten die Struktur des Moleküls.
example_2_translation: Os pesquisadores analisaram a estrutura da molécula.
example_3: Dieses Molekül ist für die Reaktion verantwortlich.
example_3_translation: Esta molécula é responsável pela reação.
pronunciation_tips: French origin; stress on last syllable: mo-le-KÜL; 'ü' = /yː/ (no English equivalent, round lips for 'ee')
memory_hook: MOLEcule = a MOLE of particles; small building block of matter
related_words: das Atom, die Verbindung, die Chemie, molekular
common_mistakes: Using 'die' instead of 'das'; forgetting the umlaut in Moleküle

secondary_meaning: gradual development or change of something
usage_notes: Used in biology (Darwinian evolution) and metaphorically for slow change over time
common_collocations: die Evolution der Arten, die Evolution des Menschen, biologische Evolution
image_search_query: human evolution biology Darwin species development
image_context: A diagram showing the stages of human evolution from early primates to modern humans
example_1: Die Evolution erklärt die Vielfalt des Lebens auf der Erde.
example_1_translation: A evolução explica a diversidade da vida na Terra.
example_2: Charles Darwin entwickelte die Theorie der Evolution.
example_2_translation: Charles Darwin desenvolveu a teoria da evolução.
example_3: Die Evolution ist ein langsamer, kontinuierlicher Prozess.
example_3_translation: A evolução é um processo lento e contínuo.
pronunciation_tips: Latin origin; stress on third syllable: e-vo-LU-tion; '-tion' = /t͡si̯oːn/ (not 'shun' as in English)
memory_hook: EVOLution = things EVOLVE slowly over many generations
related_words: evolvieren, die Selektion, die Mutation, der Darwinismus, biologisch
common_mistakes: Pronouncing '-tion' as in English; using 'der' or 'das'

usage_notes: Physics term; uncountable in most contexts; also expressed as 'Gravitation' in technical texts
common_collocations: die Schwerkraft überwinden, unter dem Einfluss der Schwerkraft, die Schwerkraft der Erde
synonyms: die Gravitation, die Erdanziehung, die Gravitationskraft
image_search_query: gravity apple falling Newton physics force
image_context: An apple falling from a tree illustrating Newton's law of gravity
example_1: Die Schwerkraft hält uns auf der Erde.
example_1_translation: A gravidade nos mantém na Terra.
example_2: Newton entdeckte das Gesetz der Schwerkraft.
example_2_translation: Newton descobriu a lei da gravidade.
example_3: Im Weltall ist die Schwerkraft sehr gering.
example_3_translation: No espaço, a gravidade é muito baixa.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: SCHWER (heavy) + KRAFT (force); stress on first syllable: SCHWER-kraft
memory_hook: SCHWER = heavy; KRAFT = force → gravity is the force that makes things heavy
related_words: die Gravitation, gravitationell, anziehen, die Masse, das Gewicht
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'Gravitation'; omitting article in compound nouns

primary_meaning: quantum physics, branch of physics describing subatomic phenomena
usage_notes: Technical academic term; uncountable; often used interchangeably with 'Quantenmechanik'
common_collocations: die Gesetze der Quantenphysik, Quantenphysik studieren, Anwendungen der Quantenphysik
antonyms: die klassische Physik, die Newtonschen Mechanik
image_search_query: quantum physics particle wave duality subatomic science
image_context: An abstract visualization of quantum particle-wave duality with interference patterns
example_1: Die Quantenphysik beschreibt das Verhalten von Teilchen auf atomarer Ebene.
example_1_translation: A física quântica descreve o comportamento das partículas em nível atômico.
example_2: Viele Phänomene lassen sich nur mit der Quantenphysik erklären.
example_2_translation: Muitos fenômenos só podem ser explicados pela física quântica.
example_3: Sie promovierte in Quantenphysik an der Universität Berlin.
example_3_translation: Ela se doutorou em física quântica na Universidade de Berlim.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: QUANTEN (quanta) + PHYSIK (physics); 'qu' = /kv/; stress on first syllable: QUAN-ten-phy-sik
memory_hook: QUANT = discrete packet of energy; physics of the smallest packets
related_words: das Quant, die Quantenmechanik, der Teilchen, die Welle, subatomar

usage_notes: Common in scientific contexts; informal short form 'Lab' is also used; plural can also be 'Labors'
common_collocations: im Labor arbeiten, das Labor einrichten, ein Labor leiten
image_search_query: laboratory science research equipment chemicals microscope
image_context: A modern laboratory with microscopes, test tubes, and scientific equipment
example_1: Die Forscher verbrachten Stunden im Labor.
example_1_translation: Os pesquisadores passaram horas no laboratório.
example_2: Das Labor ist mit modernen Geräten ausgestattet.
example_2_translation: O laboratório está equipado com equipamentos modernos.
example_3: Die Proben wurden ins Labor geschickt.
example_3_translation: As amostras foram enviadas ao laboratório.
pronunciation_tips: Latin origin; stress on second syllable: la-BOR; the final 'r' is a vocalic 'r' /ɐ̯/
memory_hook: LABORatory = where you LABOR (work hard) on science
related_words: das Laboratorium, der Laborant, laborieren, die Forschungseinrichtung

usage_notes: Very common in academic and scientific discourse; used in singular for research as a general activity
common_collocations: Forschung betreiben, Grundlagenforschung, angewandte Forschung, Forschungsergebnisse
synonyms: die Untersuchung, die Studie, die Recherche
image_search_query: research scientist laboratory data analysis study
image_context: A researcher analyzing data on a computer in a scientific setting
example_1: Die Forschung auf diesem Gebiet schreitet schnell voran.
example_1_translation: A pesquisa nesta área está avançando rapidamente.
example_2: Er widmete sein Leben der wissenschaftlichen Forschung.
example_2_translation: Ele dedicou sua vida à pesquisa científica.
example_3: Die Forschungsergebnisse wurden in einer Fachzeitschrift veröffentlicht.
example_3_translation: Os resultados da pesquisa foram publicados em uma revista científica.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable: FOR-schung; 'sch' = /ʃ/ (like 'sh' in English)
memory_hook: FORSCHEN = to search forward; research = searching ahead for new knowledge
related_words: forschen, der Forscher, die Forscherin, das Forschungszentrum, forschend
common_mistakes: Treating as uncountable; using 'Forschungen' when speaking of research in general

usage_notes: In science, refers to a well-substantiated explanation; colloquially also means speculation
common_collocations: eine Theorie entwickeln, die Relativitätstheorie, in der Theorie, Theorie und Praxis
synonyms: die Hypothese, das Modell, das Konzept
antonyms: die Praxis, die Anwendung, der Beweis
image_search_query: theory science Einstein chalkboard equations physics
image_context: A chalkboard covered in scientific equations representing a physical theory
example_1: Einstein entwickelte die allgemeine Relativitätstheorie.
example_1_translation: Einstein desenvolveu a teoria da relatividade geral.
example_2: In der Theorie klingt das gut, aber in der Praxis ist es schwierig.
example_2_translation: Na teoria isso soa bem, mas na prática é difícil.
example_3: Die Theorie wird durch zahlreiche Experimente gestützt.
example_3_translation: A teoria é sustentada por numerosos experimentos.
pronunciation_tips: Greek origin; stress on last syllable: the-o-RI; final 'e' has long /iː/ sound
memory_hook: THEORy = what THEO (the Greek thinker) proposed before the proof
related_words: theoretisch, theoretisieren, der Theoretiker, die Praxis
common_mistakes: Pronouncing final syllable as short /ɪ/ instead of long /iː/

primary_meaning: finding, insight, knowledge gained through research
usage_notes: Used in academic and philosophical contexts; 'Erkenntnisse' (plural) is very common for research findings
common_collocations: neue Erkenntnisse gewinnen, wissenschaftliche Erkenntnisse, zur Erkenntnis gelangen
synonyms: die Einsicht, der Befund, das Wissen, die Entdeckung
image_search_query: insight discovery research lightbulb moment science
image_context: A researcher experiencing a moment of discovery while reviewing data
example_1: Die neuen Erkenntnisse verändern unser Verständnis des Gehirns.
example_1_translation: Os novos achados mudam nossa compreensão do cérebro.
example_2: Die Studie führte zu wichtigen wissenschaftlichen Erkenntnissen.
example_2_translation: O estudo levou a importantes descobertas científicas.
example_3: Er gelangte zur Erkenntnis, dass seine Theorie falsch war.
example_3_translation: Ele chegou à conclusão de que sua teoria estava errada.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: er-KENNT-nis; the 'er-' prefix is unstressed /ɛɐ̯/
memory_hook: ERKENNEN = to recognize; ERKENNTNIS = what you recognize/discover
related_words: erkennen, erkennbar, die Kognition, das Wissen, erkenntnistheoretisch
common_mistakes: Using 'der' or 'das' instead of 'die'; confusing singular and plural forms

primary_meaning: atom, smallest unit of a chemical element
usage_notes: Core term in chemistry and physics; also used metaphorically for the smallest unit of something
common_collocations: das Atom spalten, ein Atom besteht aus, Atome und Moleküle
image_search_query: atom nucleus electrons protons neutrons physics model
image_context: A Bohr model illustration of an atom with nucleus and orbiting electrons
example_1: Ein Atom besteht aus einem Kern und Elektronen.
example_1_translation: Um átomo é composto por um núcleo e elétrons.
example_2: Alle Materie besteht aus Atomen.
example_2_translation: Toda a matéria é composta por átomos.
example_3: Die Atome verbinden sich zu Molekülen.
example_3_translation: Os átomos se combinam para formar moléculas.
pronunciation_tips: Greek origin; stress on first syllable: A-tom; long 'a' /aː/
memory_hook: ATOM = Greek 'atomos' meaning indivisible; the 'uncuttable' building block
related_words: atomar, der Atomkern, das Elektron, das Proton, die Atomenergie

primary_meaning: radiation, emission of energy in the form of waves or particles
usage_notes: Used in physics, medicine, and environmental contexts; can be radioactive or electromagnetic
common_collocations: radioaktive Strahlung, elektromagnetische Strahlung, Strahlung messen
image_search_query: radiation nuclear physics electromagnetic waves energy
image_context: A diagram showing different types of electromagnetic radiation on the spectrum
example_1: Radioaktive Strahlung ist für den Menschen gefährlich.
example_1_translation: A radiação radioativa é perigosa para os seres humanos.
example_2: Die Sonne sendet elektromagnetische Strahlung aus.
example_2_translation: O sol emite radiação eletromagnética.
example_3: Das Gerät misst die Strahlung in der Umgebung.
example_3_translation: O aparelho mede a radiação no ambiente.
pronunciation_tips: Compound from STRAHLEN (to radiate) + -ung suffix; stress on first syllable: STRAHL-ung
memory_hook: STRAHL = ray; STRAHLUNG = the act of sending out rays
related_words: strahlen, der Strahl, radioaktiv, die Radioaktivität, strahlend
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'Strahlen' (rays, noun plural) vs. 'Strahlung' (radiation, abstract)

usage_notes: Physics term; also used in everyday language to mean how compact or concentrated something is
common_collocations: die Dichte messen, hohe Dichte, geringe Dichte, die Bevölkerungsdichte
image_search_query: density physics liquid objects floating sinking comparison
image_context: Objects of different densities in water, some floating and some sinking
example_1: Gold hat eine sehr hohe Dichte.
example_1_translation: O ouro tem uma densidade muito alta.
example_2: Die Dichte von Wasser beträgt etwa 1 g/cm³.
example_2_translation: A densidade da água é de aproximadamente 1 g/cm³.
example_3: In diesem Stadtgebiet herrscht eine hohe Bevölkerungsdichte.
example_3_translation: Nesta área urbana há uma alta densidade populacional.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable: DICH-te; 'ch' after 'i' = /ç/ (soft, like German 'ich')
memory_hook: DICHT = tight/dense; DICHTE = the degree of tightness/density
related_words: dicht, verdichten, die Bevölkerungsdichte, die Materialdichte

primary_meaning: magnetism, physical phenomenon of magnetic fields
usage_notes: Uncountable in standard use; also used figuratively for personal charisma or attraction
common_collocations: der Elektromagnetismus, Magnetismus nutzen, der Magnetismus der Erde
image_search_query: magnetism magnetic field iron filings bar magnet physics
image_context: Iron filings arranged in a pattern around a bar magnet showing magnetic field lines
example_1: Magnetismus wird in vielen technischen Geräten genutzt.
example_1_translation: O magnetismo é utilizado em muitos dispositivos tecnológicos.
example_2: Der Elektromagnetismus ist eine der vier Grundkräfte der Natur.
example_2_translation: O eletromagnetismo é uma das quatro forças fundamentais da natureza.
example_3: Sie erforschten den Magnetismus verschiedener Materialien.
example_3_translation: Eles pesquisaram o magnetismo de diferentes materiais.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on third syllable: mag-ne-TIS-mus; Greek/Latin origin with '-ismus' suffix
memory_hook: MAGNET + -ismus (system/doctrine): the physical principle behind magnets
related_words: magnetisch, der Magnet, magnetisieren, der Elektromagnetismus
common_mistakes: Using plural form where none exists

usage_notes: Used in biology for any living entity; also used metaphorically for complex organized systems
common_collocations: ein lebender Organismus, einzelliger Organismus, der menschliche Organismus
synonyms: das Lebewesen, das Wesen, die Kreatur
image_search_query: organism biology living being microscope bacteria cell
image_context: A microscopic image of a single-celled organism under high magnification
example_1: Jeder Organismus benötigt Energie zum Überleben.
example_1_translation: Todo organismo precisa de energia para sobreviver.
example_2: Der menschliche Organismus ist extrem komplex.
example_2_translation: O organismo humano é extremamente complexo.
example_3: Diese Organismen können unter extremen Bedingungen leben.
example_3_translation: Esses organismos podem viver em condições extremas.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on third syllable: or-ga-NIS-mus; '-ismus' = /ɪsmʊs/
memory_hook: ORGAN + -ismus: an organized system of living organs
related_words: organisch, die Zelle, der Mikroorganismus, biologisch

secondary_meaning: cell (small room, prison cell), battery cell
usage_notes: Context determines meaning; in science = biological cell; in architecture = small compartment
common_collocations: die Zelle teilt sich, Zellen untersuchen, die Stammzelle, die Nervenzelle
image_search_query: biology cell microscope nucleus membrane organelles
image_context: A detailed illustration of a biological cell showing the nucleus, membrane, and organelles
example_1: Der menschliche Körper besteht aus Billionen von Zellen.
example_1_translation: O corpo humano é composto por trilhões de células.
example_2: Die Zelle teilt sich durch einen Prozess namens Mitose.
example_2_translation: A célula se divide por um processo chamado mitose.
example_3: Stammzellen haben die Fähigkeit, sich in verschiedene Zelltypen zu verwandeln.
example_3_translation: As células-tronco têm a capacidade de se transformar em diferentes tipos celulares.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable: ZEL-le; 'z' = /t͡s/ (like 'ts' in 'cats')
memory_hook: CELL in English = ZELLE in German; both from Latin 'cella' (small room)
related_words: zelluläre, die Stammzelle, die Nervenzelle, der Zellkern, teilen

usage_notes: Core term in genetics and molecular biology; used in compound words like 'Gentherapie', 'Gensequenz'
common_collocations: das Gen trägt die Information, Gene vererben, ein Gen mutiert
image_search_query: gene DNA helix genetics biology research
image_context: A double helix DNA strand with highlighted sections representing individual genes
example_1: Gene bestimmen viele Eigenschaften eines Lebewesens.
example_1_translation: Os genes determinam muitas características de um ser vivo.
example_2: Das Gen für blaue Augen wird von den Eltern vererbt.
example_2_translation: O gene para olhos azuis é herdado dos pais.
example_3: Mutationen in bestimmten Genen können Krankheiten verursachen.
example_3_translation: Mutações em certos genes podem causar doenças.
pronunciation_tips: Long 'e' /eː/; stress on single syllable: GEN; 'g' = hard /ɡ/
memory_hook: GEN = short, fundamental; like a genetic GENE that carries your traits
related_words: genetisch, die Genetik, das Genom, der Genotyp, das Erbgut

usage_notes: Used in chemistry (chemical reaction), physics, biology, and everyday language for any response
common_collocations: eine chemische Reaktion, die Kettenreaktion, Reaktion zeigen, eine Reaktion auslösen
synonyms: die Antwort, die Erwiderung, die Wirkung
image_search_query: chemical reaction laboratory test tube beaker explosion color change
image_context: A chemical reaction in a laboratory beaker showing a color change and bubbling
example_1: Bei dieser Reaktion entsteht Kohlendioxid.
example_1_translation: Nesta reação, é produzido dióxido de carbono.
example_2: Der Körper zeigt eine allergische Reaktion auf das Medikament.
example_2_translation: O corpo mostra uma reação alérgica ao medicamento.
example_3: Die Kettenreaktion lässt sich kaum kontrollieren.
example_3_translation: A reação em cadeia é difícil de controlar.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on third syllable: re-ak-TI-on; '-tion' = /t͡si̯oːn/ (not English 'shun')
memory_hook: RE-ACTION = acting back in response; a chemical 're-action'
related_words: reagieren, reaktiv, die chemische Reaktion, der Reaktor
common_mistakes: Pronouncing '-tion' as in English; using 'der' or 'das'

usage_notes: Fundamental physics term; also widely used colloquially for personal vitality and drive
common_collocations: Energie erzeugen, Energie verbrauchen, erneuerbare Energie, kinetische Energie
synonyms: die Kraft, die Leistung, die Vitalität
image_search_query: energy electricity solar wind power renewable science
image_context: Solar panels and wind turbines generating renewable energy against a blue sky
example_1: Kinetische Energie wird in potenzielle Energie umgewandelt.
example_1_translation: A energia cinética é convertida em energia potencial.
example_2: Erneuerbare Energien spielen eine wichtige Rolle im Klimaschutz.
example_2_translation: As energias renováveis desempenham um papel importante na proteção climática.
example_3: Der Körper wandelt Nahrung in Energie um.
example_3_translation: O corpo converte alimentos em energia.
pronunciation_tips: Greek origin; stress on last syllable: e-ner-GI; final 'e' = long /iː/
memory_hook: EN-ERGY: the 'work' (ergon in Greek) hidden inside matter
related_words: energetisch, energiereich, die Energiequelle, der Energieverbrauch
common_mistakes: Using 'der' or 'das'; pronouncing final syllable as short /ɪ/

usage_notes: Common in science and engineering; refers both to the process and the result of measurement
common_collocations: eine Messung durchführen, genaue Messungen, die Temperaturmessung
image_search_query: measurement ruler ruler scale laboratory precision science
image_context: A scientist taking a precise measurement with laboratory instruments
example_1: Die Messung ergab einen Wert von 42 Grad Celsius.
example_1_translation: A medição resultou em um valor de 42 graus Celsius.
example_2: Für genaue Messungen braucht man präzise Instrumente.
example_2_translation: Para medições precisas, são necessários instrumentos de precisão.
example_3: Die Messungen wurden mehrfach wiederholt.
example_3_translation: As medições foram repetidas várias vezes.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable: MESS-ung; double 's' = /s/, not /z/
memory_hook: MESSEN = to measure; MESSUNG = the measuring act/result
related_words: messen, der Messwert, das Messgerät, die Präzision

primary_meaning: observation, the act of observing systematically
usage_notes: Key term in the scientific method; refers to systematic watching and recording of phenomena
common_collocations: Beobachtungen machen, genaue Beobachtung, die Beobachtung bestätigen
image_search_query: observation scientist telescope microscope watching nature
image_context: A scientist looking through a telescope making careful observations of celestial objects
example_1: Genaue Beobachtungen sind die Grundlage jeder wissenschaftlichen Methode.
example_1_translation: Observações precisas são a base de todo método científico.
example_2: Die Beobachtung des Verhaltens von Tieren liefert wichtige Daten.
example_2_translation: A observação do comportamento animal fornece dados importantes.
example_3: Er notierte seine Beobachtungen sorgfältig im Notizbuch.
example_3_translation: Ele anotou cuidadosamente suas observações no caderno.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: be-OB-ach-tung; 'ch' after 'a' = /x/ (guttural, like 'Bach')
memory_hook: BEOBACHTEN = to observe/watch; BEOBACHTUNG = the watching process
related_words: beobachten, der Beobachter, beobachtbar, die Überwachung

usage_notes: Widely used in science, economics, and everyday contexts; the '-se' ending is characteristic of Greek-derived nouns
common_collocations: eine Analyse durchführen, die Datenanalyse, Analyse und Synthese, statistische Analyse
image_search_query: data analysis graph chart scientist research results
image_context: A researcher analyzing data charts and graphs on multiple computer screens
example_1: Die Analyse der Daten ergab interessante Muster.
example_1_translation: A análise dos dados revelou padrões interessantes.
example_2: Eine gründliche Analyse ist vor jeder Entscheidung notwendig.
example_2_translation: Uma análise cuidadosa é necessária antes de qualquer decisão.
example_3: Die chemische Analyse zeigte Spuren von Blei.
example_3_translation: A análise química mostrou traços de chumbo.
pronunciation_tips: Greek origin; stress on third syllable: a-na-LY-se; 'y' = /yː/ (round lips for 'ee')
memory_hook: ANALYSE = to undo (ana = up/back) and lyse (loosen) a complex thing into parts
related_words: analysieren, der Analytiker, analytisch, die Synthese
common_mistakes: Using 'der' or 'das'; mispronouncing 'y' as /iː/ instead of /yː/

usage_notes: In biology: bacterial or viral strain; also means tree trunk or tribal lineage; context determines meaning
common_collocations: ein resistenter Stamm, der Baumstamm, der Stammbaum
synonyms: der Klon, die Linie, das Gesicht
image_search_query: bacterial strain culture biology petri dish research microbiology
image_context: Bacterial colonies growing in a petri dish representing a biological strain
example_1: Dieser Stamm des Virus ist besonders ansteckend.
example_1_translation: Esta cepa do vírus é particularmente contagiosa.
example_2: Die Forscher isolierten einen neuen Stamm des Bakteriums.
example_2_translation: Os pesquisadores isolaram uma nova cepa da bactéria.
example_3: Der Stammbaum zeigt die Verwandtschaft der verschiedenen Arten.
example_3_translation: A árvore genealógica mostra o parentesco das diferentes espécies.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on single syllable: STAMM; 'st' at start = /ʃt/ (not English 'st')
memory_hook: STAMM = trunk of tree; in biology, the 'trunk' or root line of a strain
related_words: der Stammbaum, die Stammzelle, abstammen, der Ursprung

primary_meaning: synthesis, combining elements to form a compound or whole
usage_notes: Used in chemistry for producing compounds; in philosophy and logic for combining thesis and antithesis
common_collocations: die chemische Synthese, eine Synthese herstellen, Analyse und Synthese
synonyms: die Verbindung, die Kombination, die Zusammenfassung
image_search_query: chemical synthesis laboratory flask reaction organic chemistry
image_context: A chemist synthesizing a compound in a laboratory with round-bottom flasks
example_1: Die Synthese des Medikaments erfordert mehrere chemische Schritte.
example_1_translation: A síntese do medicamento requer várias etapas químicas.
example_2: In der Dialektik folgt die Synthese auf die Antithese.
example_2_translation: Na dialética, a síntese segue a antítese.
example_3: Die Forscher entwickelten eine neue Methode zur Synthese des Proteins.
example_3_translation: Os pesquisadores desenvolveram um novo método para a síntese da proteína.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable: SYN-the-se; 'y' = /ʏ/ (short, rounded); 's' at start = /z/
memory_hook: SYN (together) + THESIS (placing): putting things together to form a whole
related_words: synthetisieren, synthetisch, die Analyse, die Photosynthese

primary_meaning: catalyst, substance that accelerates a chemical reaction without being consumed
usage_notes: Used literally in chemistry and figuratively to describe anything that accelerates a process or change
common_collocations: als Katalysator wirken, der chemische Katalysator, ein Katalysator für Veränderungen
image_search_query: catalyst chemistry reaction enzyme accelerator molecule
image_context: A diagram showing a chemical reaction being accelerated by a catalyst molecule
example_1: Das Enzym wirkt als Katalysator in dieser biologischen Reaktion.
example_1_translation: A enzima atua como catalisador nesta reação biológica.
example_2: Die Krise diente als Katalysator für politische Reformen.
example_2_translation: A crise serviu como catalisador para reformas políticas.
example_3: Ohne Katalysator würde die Reaktion viel länger dauern.
example_3_translation: Sem o catalisador, a reação levaria muito mais tempo.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on fourth syllable: ka-ta-ly-SA-tor; 'y' = /yː/; final '-or' = /oːɐ̯/
memory_hook: CATA (down) + LYZE (loosen): breaks down the energy barrier to speed up reactions
related_words: katalysieren, katalytisch, der Inhibitor, die Enzym
